# dotmatter

> Interactive particle-field image effects for React. dotmatter turns any image, video, canvas, or headline text into a field of physically simulated particles rendered with WebGL2. Every particle has a home position, velocity, and spring — the cursor pushes (or attracts) them and they bounce back with a fluid overshoot. Ten visual effects (dither, particles, halftone, ascii, led, stitch, scanline, mosaic, rings, contour) share the same physics. Everything renders locally in the browser; nothing is uploaded.

This file is complete standalone documentation for AI agents. You do not need to fetch any other page to integrate dotmatter.

- Package: `@dotmatter/react` on npm (current: 0.3.1). Peer dep: `react` >= 19. Automatically pulls in `@dotmatter/core` and `@dotmatter/shaders` — install only `@dotmatter/react`.
- Repo: https://github.com/Tresnanda/dotmatter
- License: MIT
- Requires: WebGL2 (all modern browsers). No SSR rendering — the component renders an `<img>` fallback until it mounts, so it is safe in Next.js/Remix as long as it runs as a client component (`"use client"`).

## Install

```bash
npm install @dotmatter/react
# or: pnpm add / yarn add / bun add @dotmatter/react
```

## Quick start (copy this)

```tsx
"use client" // if using Next.js App Router

import { DotMatter } from "@dotmatter/react"
import { particleEffect } from "@dotmatter/shaders"

export function Hero() {
  return (
    <DotMatter
      src="/photo.jpg"
      effect={particleEffect}
      alt="A description of the image"
      style={{ width: "100%", aspectRatio: "16 / 10" }}
    />
  )
}
```

Critical layout rule: the component fills its own box. YOU must give it a size — set `width` + `aspect-ratio` (or explicit height) via `style` or `className`. A zero-height container renders nothing.

## If it's not showing (read this FIRST when integrating)

Ranked by how often each one is the culprit:

1. NO SIZE / ZERO HEIGHT — the most common cause of a blank result. The component has no intrinsic size of its own beyond what its source gives it. ALWAYS set an explicit size on the component:
   `<DotMatter ... style={{ width: "100%", aspectRatio: "16 / 10" }} />`
   For image/video sources it can borrow the media's aspect ratio, but being explicit is bulletproof. For `DotMatterText` and canvas sources, a size is effectively required. If you drop it into a flex or grid cell, that cell can collapse it to 0 — give it a width/height or `flex: 1` + a min-height.

2. NEXT.JS / RSC — the file must be a Client Component. Add `"use client"` at the very top of the file that renders `<DotMatter>`. In a Server Component the effect never runs, so you get the static fallback image and no particles (looks "dead"). If you only ever see the plain image, this is very likely why.

3. CROSS-ORIGIN IMAGES (blank canvas / console CORS error) — the image is uploaded to a WebGL texture, so it must be same-origin OR served with `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`. A hotlinked image from another domain without CORS headers taints the canvas and the effect fails (the fallback `<img>` still shows). Host the image yourself or use a CORS-enabled CDN.

4. WEBGL2 UNAVAILABLE — rare, but on very old browsers or locked-down environments WebGL2 may be off. `onError` fires and the plain `<img>`/`<video>` stays visible, so it degrades gracefully rather than breaking the page. Pass `onError` to log it if you need to know.

If you still only see a static image (no motion): it's almost always #1 (no height, so the canvas has 0 pixels to draw into) or #2 (`"use client"` missing). Check those two before anything else.

(Note: dotmatter >= 0.3.1 fixes a bug where cached/preloaded images sometimes never initialized the effect. If you were on an earlier version and the effect was intermittent on repeat visits, upgrade.)

## Components

### `<DotMatter />` — the main component

```tsx
import { DotMatter } from "@dotmatter/react"
```

Props:
- `src: string | HTMLCanvasElement` (required) — image URL, video URL (with `video`), or a canvas element used directly as the texture.
- `effect: EffectDefinition` (required) — one of the ten effect objects imported from `@dotmatter/shaders` (see Effects below).
- `alt: string` (required) — accessible description; the original `<img>` stays in the DOM as fallback.
- `video?: boolean` — set true when `src` is a video URL. Renders a muted, looped, autoplaying `<video>` streamed to the GPU every frame.
- `preset?: string` — named preset for the chosen effect (see Effects table).
- `effectOptions?: Record<string, unknown>` — per-effect uniform overrides, plus universal options (see Options below). Live-updates without rebuilding.
- `ambient?: AmbientMotion` — idle drift: `{ mode: "wave" | "breathe" | "flow" | "jitter", strength?: number, speed?: number, scale?: number }`. Strength ~0.3–1.0 is tasteful. Omit for a still field.
- `pointerMode?: "repel" | "attract"` — default `"repel"`. Attract pulls particles toward the cursor instead.
- `scrollReveal?: "auto" | number` — scroll-driven assembly. `"auto"`: particles start scattered and condense into the image as the element scrolls into view (fully interruptible, reverses on scroll-up). A number 0–1 drives progress manually (0 = scattered, 1 = assembled).
- `reduceMotion?: boolean` — gentle mode: no ambient drift, no reveal scatter, calmer springs. Defaults to the user's `prefers-reduced-motion` setting; only pass this to override.
- `controls?: (c: { capture(): string } | null) => void` — receives imperative controls once live. `capture()` returns the current frame as a PNG data URL.
- `onError?: (error: Error & { code?: string }) => void` — e.g. WebGL2 unavailable. The plain `<img>`/`<video>` remains visible on failure, so failure is graceful by default.
- `className?`, `style?` — applied to the wrapper `<span>` (display: block). Note the wrapper sets `touch-action: none` so touch drags drive the field; override via `style` if you need touch scrolling over it.

### Flagship shorthands (no `effect` prop needed)

```tsx
import { DitherImage, ParticleImage, HalftoneImage } from "@dotmatter/react"
<ParticleImage src="/photo.jpg" alt="..." />
```

Same props as `DotMatter` minus `effect`.

### `<DotMatterText />` — headlines as particle fields

```tsx
import { DotMatterText } from "@dotmatter/react"
import { particleEffect } from "@dotmatter/shaders"

<DotMatterText
  text="HELLO"
  effect={particleEffect}
  font="700 200px system-ui, sans-serif" // optional, this is the default
  style={{ width: "100%", aspectRatio: "3.2" }}
/>
```

Rasterizes the text to an offscreen canvas and feeds it through the same pipeline. Particles are allocated only inside the glyphs (alpha masking). The real text stays in the DOM, visually hidden, for screen readers and SEO. Accepts `effect`, `preset`, `effectOptions`, `ambient`, `pointerMode`, `scrollReveal`, `reduceMotion`, `controls`, `fill` (text color used for luminance sampling, default white).

## Effects

Import from `@dotmatter/shaders`. Every effect is the same particle simulation with a different way of drawing each particle.

| Import | id | Look | Presets |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `particleEffect` | `particles` | Soft luminance-sized dots (the signature look) | `soft-repel`, `dense-field` |
| `ditherEffect` | `dither` | Bayer-matrix 1-bit dither tiles, two-ink palette | `editorial`, `terminal` |
| `halftoneEffect` | `halftone` | Print-style halftone dots (RGB separation in source mode) | `mono-newsprint`, `cmyk-print` |
| `asciiEffect` | `ascii` | Procedural glyph ramp, terminal green by default | `terminal`, `typewriter` |
| `ledEffect` | `led` | Glowing LED pixels | `stadium`, `soft-billboard` |
| `stitchEffect` | `stitch` | Cross-stitch X marks | `embroidery`, `fine-thread` |
| `scanlineEffect` | `scanline` | Horizontal CRT bars | `crt`, `receipt` |
| `mosaicEffect` | `mosaic` | Rounded mosaic tiles | `tiles`, `fine-grid` |
| `ringsEffect` | `rings` | Concentric engraved rings | `engraving`, `medallion` |
| `contourEffect` | `contour` | Gradient-following strokes, sketch-like | `sketch`, `cross-hatch` |

## effectOptions

All numeric options live-update. Changing `spacing` rebuilds the particle grid (still cheap).

Universal (every effect):
- `spacing` — px between particles at DPR 1. Lower = denser = more particles. Typical 5–16.
- `contrast` (0–3, default ~1), `hue` (-180–180 degrees), `saturation` (0–2), `exposure` (-2–2) — image adjustments applied on the GPU.
- `colorMode` — `0` = colors sampled from the source image, `1` = flat tint. Each effect has a sensible default.
- `tint` — hex color string, used when `colorMode: 1` (dither uses `colorDark`/`colorLight` two-ink palette instead).
- Physics: `force` (push strength, default 16), `forceRadius` (cursor influence radius in normalized units, default ~0.085 — small and precise), `spring` (return stiffness, default 26), `damping` (default 0.92; lower = bouncier).
- `alphaThreshold` — for transparent PNGs: particles are only created where source alpha exceeds this, so a cut-out subject becomes its own interactive silhouette.

Per-effect extras (main ones): dither `scale` + `threshold` + `colorDark`/`colorLight`; halftone dot behavior via `spacing`/`contrast`; led `glow` (0–2).

Example — tinted, denser, bouncier:

```tsx
<DotMatter
  src="/logo.png"
  effect={particleEffect}
  alt="Logo"
  effectOptions={{
    spacing: 8,
    colorMode: 1,
    tint: "#d8ff36",
    force: 22,
    damping: 0.88,
  }}
/>
```

## Recipes

Scroll-reveal hero (particles assemble as the section scrolls in):

```tsx
<DotMatter src="/hero.jpg" effect={halftoneEffect} alt="Hero" scrollReveal="auto"
  style={{ width: "100%", aspectRatio: "16/9" }} />
```

Video source:

```tsx
<DotMatter src="/clip.mp4" video effect={ledEffect} alt="Showreel"
  style={{ width: "100%", aspectRatio: "16/9" }} />
```

Transparent-PNG silhouette that attracts particles to the cursor:

```tsx
<DotMatter src="/cutout.png" effect={particleEffect} alt="Product"
  pointerMode="attract" effectOptions={{ alphaThreshold: 0.5 }}
  style={{ width: 480, aspectRatio: "1" }} />
```

PNG export button:

```tsx
const ref = useRef<{ capture(): string } | null>(null)
<DotMatter src="/a.jpg" effect={ditherEffect} alt="A" controls={(c) => (ref.current = c)} />
<button onClick={() => { const url = ref.current?.capture(); /* download url */ }}>Save PNG</button>
```

## Custom effects (advanced)

`@dotmatter/core` exports `defineEffect` for writing your own particle skin: provide `id`, `geometry: { type: "particles" }`, GLSL vertex/fragment shaders, `uniforms` (with defaults/min/max), and optional `presets`. Study any effect in the `@dotmatter/shaders` source as a template — they are all built on the public API. Core also exports the renderer (`createShaderImageRenderer`) for non-React use: methods `render(frame)`, `resize`, `setSource`, `updateEffectOptions`, `setAmbient`, `setReveal`, `setPointerMode`, `capture`, `destroy`.

## Gotchas for agents

1. Always size the component (`width` + `aspect-ratio`); it cannot infer a height from the container alone.
2. It's a client component — add `"use client"` in Next.js App Router files that use it.
3. `alt` is required on `DotMatter`; keep it meaningful (the fallback `<img>` uses it).
4. Cross-origin images need CORS headers (`crossorigin`-clean sources) or the WebGL texture upload fails — same-origin or properly-CORS'd URLs only.
5. `touch-action: none` is set on the wrapper: on mobile, touching the canvas moves particles instead of scrolling. If the element is large on mobile, pass `style={{ touchAction: "pan-y" }}` to keep page scrolling.
6. Don't recreate `effect`/`effectOptions` objects every render for no reason — `effectOptions` is diffed live, but the `effect` object identity triggers a full renderer rebuild. Import effects at module scope.
7. Reduced motion is automatic; do not build your own `prefers-reduced-motion` wrapper around it.
